rulings.Case Title: 75 HOWARD OWNER, LP VS. KBSIII 201 SPEAR STREET, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT 201 SPEAR PROPERTY, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact exists. See Plaintiff's response to Separate Statement. The Court additionally notes that according to the Notice of Motion Defendant solely seeks summary judgment/adjudication "as to the operative complaint filed by Plaintiff." Defendant thereafter discusses issues and advances arguments which appear to be affirmative defenses (e.g. laches) and affirmative claims (equitable easement). Neither the Defendant's Answer (and affirmative defenses therein) nor the Cross-Complaint is mentioned in the Notice of Motion. However, it appears from the opposition that Plaintiffs do not take any issue with this procedural defect and address the matters on the merits (e.g. "The third cause of action in KBS's Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("SAXC") for declaratory relief alleges KBS has an equitable easement over that portion of 75 Howard's property where the sheet piles are encroaching. SAXC, 42-46." Opposition 11:22-24). Therefore, the Court considered and denied this motion on the merits. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PRITCHARD HILL PROPERTIES LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL VS. JUPITER COLONY, LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT JUPITER COLONY, LLC, BRAND NAPA VALLEY, LLC, EDWARD FITTS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for the cause of action for declaratory relief to state a present actual controversy that does not involve past wrongs/breached for which Plaintiff has an adequate remedy at all, e.g. a declaration of parties' rights and obligations under the contract going forward. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Causes of action for negligent misrepresentation and concealment are not duplicative of the cause of action for intentional misrepresentation. Delayed discovery is sufficiently alleged. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. If Plaintiff fails to timely file an amended complaint, Defendant shall file an answer within 20 days of notice of entry of this order in response to the complaint without a cause of action for declaratory relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
